creator: russ.haley@gmail.com
text:
I've been having intermittent network problems on a SuperMicro Server. My computer is exhibiting a similar pathology as Mr. Sergey V. Dyatkos'.

I'm serving Minecraft to an internal network over a dlink powerline ethernet modem. It seems over the last few days the powerline network has been going up and down, which eventually causes the "TX(5) desc avail = 1024" error. My system uses the igb driver.

russellh@sylvester:~> uname -a
FreeBSD sylvester 12.0-RELEASE-p3 FreeBSD 12.0-RELEASE-p3 r346787 GENERIC  amd64

russellh@sylvester:~> pciconf -lv
#SNIP#
igb0@pci0:2:0:0:        class=0x020000 card=0x10c915d9 chip=0x10c98086 rev=0x01 hdr=0x00
    vendor     = 'Intel Corporation'
    device     = '82576 Gigabit Network Connection'
    class      = network
    subclass   = ethernet
